title: T-114 — GDPR erasure cron job in apps/worker + gdpr_erasure_requests migration
prompt:
# T-114 — GDPR erasure cron job in apps/worker

PRD: docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md §13 Compliance ("GDPR right-to-erasure function działa"), §11 Audit log (retention_class=security, 7y), §10 (R13)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Add a scheduled job to the `apps/worker` JobRegistry (T-111) that picks pending rows from a new `gdpr_erasure_requests` table and invokes `runErasure()` from `@monopilot/gdpr` (T-113). This closes the §13 success criterion at the **runtime** layer (T-113 owned the framework; this task owns the caller).

## Implementation contract
1. Create Drizzle migration `packages/db/migrations/0NNN-gdpr-erasure-requests.sql` (verify next NNN by `/bin/ls packages/db/migrations/`) defining:
   ```
   CREATE TABLE gdpr_erasure_requests (
     id           UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
     org_id       UUID NOT NULL,
     subject_id   UUID NOT NULL,
     requested_by UUID NOT NULL,
     requested_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
     status       TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'
                  CHECK (status IN ('pending','running','completed','failed','cancelled')),
     started_at   TIMESTAMPTZ,
     completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
     domains_run  TEXT[] NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
     last_error   TEXT,
     created_at   TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
     updated_at   TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
   );
   ALTER TABLE gdpr_erasure_requests ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
   ALTER TABLE gdpr_erasure_requests FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
   CREATE POLICY gdpr_erasure_requests_org_context ON gdpr_erasure_requests
     USING (org_id = app.current_org_id())
     WITH CHECK (org_id = app.current_org_id());
   ```
   Plus Drizzle schema entry `packages/db/src/schema/gdpr.ts`.
2. Create `apps/worker/src/jobs/gdpr-erasure-cron.ts` exporting `registerGdprErasureCron(registry, opts)`. Default schedule: every 60s interval. Handler logic:
   - `SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` one row WHERE status='pending' LIMIT 1, set status='running' + started_at=now().
   - Call `runErasure(pool, row.org_id, row.subject_id, { dryRun: false })`.
   - On success: UPDATE status='completed', completed_at=now(), domains_run=report.results.map(r=>r.domain).
   - On failure: UPDATE status='failed', last_error=err.message.
   - Repeat until no pending rows OR maxPerTick reached (default 5).
3. Register the job in `apps/worker/src/index.ts` after the outbox consumer.
4. Add `apps/worker/src/jobs/__tests__/gdpr-erasure-cron.test.ts` (testcontainers Postgres):
   - Apply audit migration + this task's migration.
   - Register a fake erasure handler in @monopilot/gdpr that touches a test table.
   - INSERT one pending erasure_requests row.
   - Invoke the job via `registry.runOnceForTest`.
   - Assert the row transitions to status='completed' with non-empty `domains_run` and `completed_at IS NOT NULL`.
   - INSERT a second request where handler throws; assert status='failed' + last_error populated.

## Files in scope
- packages/db/migrations/0NNN-gdpr-erasure-requests.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/gdpr.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/index.ts [modify — export gdpr schema]
- apps/worker/src/jobs/gdpr-erasure-cron.ts [create]
- apps/worker/src/jobs/__tests__/gdpr-erasure-cron.test.ts [create]
- apps/worker/src/index.ts [modify — register cron]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the migration runs, when \d gdpr_erasure_requests is inspected, then all listed columns + RLS+FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY enabled + policy uses app.current_org_id() function.
2. Given a pending row exists, when cron tick runs, then status transitions pending→running→completed in one tick and domains_run contains every registered domain name.
3. Given a registered erasure handler throws, when cron processes the row, then status pending→running→failed and last_error is non-null text.
4. Given two pending rows, when one tick runs with maxPerTick=5, then both rows end with status='completed'; AND Given a row with status='running' from a prior crashed tick, when cron runs, then SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED does not re-process it.
## Test strategy
- RED: write `gdpr-erasure-cron.test.ts` with the 5 scenarios; testcontainers Postgres applies migrations. RED command: `pnpm --filter @monopilot/worker test`.
- GREEN: implement migration + Drizzle schema + cron job + registration.
- Run: `pnpm --filter @monopilot/db migrate && pnpm --filter @monopilot/worker test`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not call `runErasure` from a Server Action — the request-path caller is forbidden; cron only.
- Do not bypass `SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` — multiple worker replicas must not collide on the same pending row.
- Do not skip the `app.set_org_context` call inside the dispatcher — RLS isolation per row is mandatory.
- Do not mark a row 'completed' if the handler partial-failed — failed-and-rolled-back means status='failed', not 'completed'.
- Do not delete completed rows — retain for audit; cleanup is governed by retention policy.
- Do not log the subject_id in stdout — PII; redaction lands via T-117 logger.

pipeline_inputs.description: Drizzle migration for gdpr_erasure_requests (with RLS) + apps/worker cron job that picks pending rows via SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED and invokes @monopilot/gdpr runErasure. Closes §13 success criterion runtime layer.
pipeline_inputs.details:
Migration creates gdpr_erasure_requests with status state machine (pending→running→completed|failed|cancelled), domains_run TEXT[], last_error TEXT, RLS via app.current_org_id() function (non-spoofable foundation contract). Cron handler picks one pending row, transitions to running, calls runErasure, transitions to completed (success) or failed (handler threw). maxPerTick default 5. testcontainers Postgres covers state-transition assertions.

AC count consolidated from 5→4 by Fixer F7 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost).
out_of_scope: Per-domain handler implementations beyond what T-113 already accepts
acceptance_criteria: Given the migration runs, when \d gdpr_erasure_requests is inspected, then all listed columns + RLS+FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY enabled + policy uses app.current_org_id() function.
Given a pending row exists, when cron tick runs, then status transitions pending→running→completed in one tick and domains_run contains every registered domain name.
Given a registered erasure handler throws, when cron processes the row, then status pending→running→failed and last_error is non-null text.

Given two pending rows, when one tick runs with maxPerTick=5, then both rows end with status='completed'; AND Given a row with status='running' from a prior crashed tick, when cron runs, then SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED does not re-process it.
test_strategy: RED: write gdpr-erasure-cron.test.ts with 5 scenarios using testcontainers Postgres + applied migrations; RED command: pnpm --filter @monopilot/worker test.
GREEN: implement migration + Drizzle schema + cron job + registration.
risk_red_lines: Do not call runErasure from a Server Action — cron only.
Do not bypass SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED — multi-replica safety.
Do not skip app.set_org_context inside dispatcher — RLS isolation per row mandatory.
Do not mark a row 'completed' if handler partial-failed — failed=failed.
Do not delete completed rows — retain for audit.
Do not log subject_id in stdout — PII redaction lands in T-117.
